cases.next_action: Write one focused test through the public submission seam: submit the worker job, drive the deterministic scheduler, then call wait_for(lambda: job.status == "complete", timeout=bounded_timeout, description="job to complete"). Run it before production implementation, make the smallest change, then refactor only while green.
cases.proof: RED must be an observed timeout or wrong final status caused by the missing completion behavior. GREEN requires the focused command passing through the public seam, followed by the nearest relevant test group. After each refactor move, rerun the focused test and finish with the relevant group.
cases.claim_boundary: May claim TDD completion only after observed RED, GREEN, relevant regression proof, and recorded refactor and residual-risk evidence; may not use sleeps or an after-the-fact passing test as RED.

cases.next_action: Write a focused fake-clock test that first observes DEBOUNCE_STARTED, records its timestamp, advances to just before DEBOUNCE_INTERVAL and proves SEARCH_REQUESTED is absent, then advances the remainder and proves one SEARCH_REQUESTED event whose timestamp is exactly start plus DEBOUNCE_INTERVAL.
cases.proof: Run before implementation and observe failure for early, late, duplicate, or missing search behavior. After the smallest production change, require the focused test and nearest relevant group to pass; the independent oracle is the settled interval constant and observed event timestamps.
cases.claim_boundary: May claim the exact timing behavior only; may not replace deterministic clock evidence with an arbitrary sleep or merely prove that the request eventually occurs.

cases.next_action: Hand off to $diagnosing-bugs because the cause and trusted red-capable reproduction are uncertain.
cases.proof: Require diagnosis to establish the exact symptom and cause plus trusted regression proof, or return a decision-needed packet.
cases.claim_boundary: TDD must not mutate production code or claim a regression cycle yet; diagnosis owns investigation, while implementation waits for an authorized, red-capable reproduction.

cases.next_action: Treat the setup error as invalid RED, repair the event-loop fixture or test setup, and rerun the focused test before changing production code.
cases.proof: A valid RED must execute the behavior and fail for the expected missing or wrong behavior rather than fixture setup.
cases.claim_boundary: May claim only that the harness is broken; may not claim observed RED or begin GREEN implementation until the repaired test fails behaviorally.

cases.next_action: Record the focused failure as valid expected RED and classify the other failure as a documented pre-existing unrelated baseline failure. Implement minimally, rerun the focused test, and rerun the nearest group while preserving both results.
cases.proof: Require observed focused GREEN plus evidence that the remaining group failure matches the documented baseline and is unrelated, with the failed relevant validation recorded as residual risk.
cases.claim_boundary: May claim the focused behavior now passes. Because the nearest relevant group is not fully green, may not claim full TDD completion, a clean relevant regression result, or that the baseline was repaired.

cases.next_action: Preserve the correct retry-count assertion and keep the repository-owned scheduler real. Fix the behavior through the chosen seam; if the seam cannot be reached safely, return the support gap or establish an authorized protected prefactor instead of weakening proof.
cases.proof: Require the original assertion to pass through owned in-process code, followed by the nearest relevant test group. Any boundary double must pass the fidelity gate and replace only a real external adapter.
cases.claim_boundary: May not weaken a correct oracle, mock the owned scheduler for convenience, or claim GREEN from a less demanding assertion.

cases.next_action: Use a real or local queue substitute capable of the consumed-timeout behavior, or improve the boundary fake and run the same adapter contract against the fake and real or local implementation.
cases.proof: Require contract evidence that success and the consumed timeout failure mode match the real boundary. If no contract comparison is available, explicitly record the unverified fidelity risk and obtain separate higher-fidelity proof for the timeout path.
cases.claim_boundary: The current fake may support only successful-receipt evidence; it cannot prove timeout behavior or justify an undisclosed production-equivalence claim.

cases.next_action: Refactor only behavior-preserving code inside the chosen tracer-bullet slice, one meaningful move at a time. Stop before the broader seam redesign and return it as residual follow-up; recommend $codebase-design for the already-framed seam question.
cases.proof: Keep the focused test green after every move and run the nearest relevant group before leaving the tracer bullet.
cases.claim_boundary: May claim only the bounded cleanup proved under the current contract; may not widen scope, alter the interface without a new RED cycle, mutate a tracker, or claim the broader redesign is complete.

cases.next_action: Refuse the completion claim and return an incomplete proof decision until the full evidence packet exists.
cases.proof: Minimum packet: Source Trace with behavior, source, seam, and independent oracle; RED command, observed failing result, and why it is the expected behavioral failure; GREEN command and passing result; relevant validation or justified skip; material refactor or none; and residual risk or blocker.
cases.claim_boundary: An expected but unobserved RED proves neither TDD nor completion; no completion claim is allowed without observed command results, GREEN, relevant regression evidence, and residual-risk disclosure.
